item.title: OpenAI co-founder Greg Brockman takes charge of product strategy
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: OpenAI's latest shakeup comes as the company reportedly plans to combine ChatGPT and its programming product Codex.

item.title: The OpenAI trial wraps up, and the Musk founder machine keeps spinning
item.dc:creator: Kirsten Korosec, Sean O'Kane, Anthony Ha, Theresa Loconsolo
item.description: The Musk v. Altman trial came to a close this week, and the final arguments kept circling back to one question: can we trust the people in charge of AI? All of this is playing out as SpaceX charges toward what could be one of the largest IPOs in American history, with a whole generation of founders already spinning out […]

item.title: Silicon Valley’s vacationland needs a new energy provider just as AI is driving prices up
item.dc:creator: Tim De Chant
item.description: Lake Tahoe, Silicon Valley's favorite vacation spot, is about to get hit with higher energy prices as AI drives demand for electricity.

item.title: OpenAI launches ChatGPT for personal finance, will let you connect bank accounts
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: Once users connect their accounts, they will see a dashboard of their portfolio performance, spending, subscriptions, and upcoming payments.

item.title: Runway started by helping filmmakers — now it wants to beat Google at AI
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: AI video-generation startup Runway is betting that video generation is the path to world models. And that being an AI outsider is an advantage, not a liability.

item.title: Osaurus brings both local and cloud AI models to your Mac
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Osaurus￼ combines local and cloud AI models in a Mac app that keeps users’ memory, files, and tools on their own hardware.

item.title: What the jury will actually decide in the case of Elon Musk vs. Sam Altman
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: Here's what the biggest tech court case of the year is all about.

item.title: Elon Musk’s SpaceXAI has been bleeding staff since its merger
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: More than 50 employees have reportedly left Elon Musk’s newly merged SpaceXAI since February, raising questions about burnout, leadership changes, talent poaching, and whether liquidity events weakened retention incentives.

item.title: OpenAI says Codex is coming to your phone
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: The update gives users enhanced flexibility over how they can manage their workflows.

item.title: What happens when AI starts building itself?
item.dc:creator: Russell Brandom
item.description: Richard Socher's new $650 million startup wants to build an AI that can research and improve itself indefinitely — and he insists it will actually ship products.

item.title: OpenAI is reportedly preparing legal action against Apple; it wouldn’t be the first partner to feel burned
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: OpenAI is so frustrated with Apple over a ChatGPT integration that failed to deliver the subscribers and prominence it expected that the company is now actively exploring legal action against the iPhone maker.

item.title: Clawdmeter turns your Claude Code usage stats into a tiny desktop dashboard
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: A new open source gadget called Clawdmeter turns Claude Code usage stats into a tiny desktop dashboard for AI coding power users.

item.title: Cerebras raises $5.5B, then stock pops $108%, in the first huge tech IPO of 2026
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: A year ago, it looked like this day would never happen for Cerebras.

item.title: Khosla Ventures is betting $10M on Ian Crosby, whose first startup, Bench, imploded
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: Crosby is building Synthetic, a fully autonomous AI bookkeeping service for other startups.

item.title: Cisco cuts nearly 4,000 jobs to spend more on AI, reports ‘record quarterly revenue’
item.dc:creator: Zack Whittaker
item.description: This is Cisco's latest layoff in recent years, while the company's chief executive touts record revenue and growth.

item.title: Two weeks left: Startup Battlefield 200 applications close May 27
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch Events
item.description: Your shot at VC access, global visibility, TechCrunch coverage, and $100K equity-free funding is running out. Deadline to apply is May 27. Apply now.

item.title: Wirestock raises $23M to supply creative multimodal data to AI labs
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: The company pivoted to being a data provider in 2023 and now supplies datasets of images, videos, design assets, and gaming and 3D content to AI labs.

item.title: Who decides what AI tells you? Campbell Brown, once Meta’s news chief, has thoughts
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: "The conversation is sort of happening in Silicon Valley around one thing, and a totally different conversation is happening among consumers," Campbell Brown said at StrictlyVC.

item.title: Clio’s $500M milestone arrives just as Anthropic ups the ante
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: Legal tech startups, including Clio, which just hit $500 million in ARR, are seeing massive customer adoption.

item.title: Notion just turned its workspace into a hub for AI agents
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Notion’s new developer platform lets teams connect AI agents, external data sources, and custom code directly into their workspace as the company pushes deeper into agentic productivity software.
